mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of periods where INDPRO increases month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above the 2.5% annual threshold forces expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5% YoY, with a t-statistic below 1.5 for the negative relationship.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, supporting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over 60-day horizons in backtests spanning 2010-2017.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue potential, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 growth against 20-day forward returns falls below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Stagnant industrial production combined with elevated inflation creates stagflationary pressures that erode profit margins and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistically significant negative correlation with the stagflation trigger condition over a 5-year sample.
